Empires & Puzzles Vlad Review: Is This 3-Star Vampire Worth Leveling?

Vlad offers Very Fast mana and a unique healing steal mechanic, but does this classic Halloween hero have enough bite for the modern meta? We break down his stats, skills, and why he might be more 'trick' than 'treat' in today's game.
Vlad Review: The Vampire Who Needs a Blood Transfusion
Vlad: The Count of Zero Impact?
Vlad is a 3-star Rare hero from the
(Halloween) event. As a member of the spooky season cast, he brings a classic vampire aesthetic and a kit centered around attrition and preventing enemy sustain. He operates at
mana speed, which is usually a hallmark of powerful heroes, but Vlad comes from an older era of
. While his concept—a healing-stealing duelist—sounds great on paper, he struggles to make a dent in the modern landscape.

Vlad sports the quintessential "Hollywood Vampire" look: pale skin, open shirt revealing an unnecessary amount of chest (and a dagger necklace?), and glowing red eyes. It’s a classic design that fits the Halloween theme perfectly, though he looks perhaps a bit too confident for someone whose bite is often softer than a mosquito's.
To put it bluntly, Vlad’s stats are
and an attack stat of just
, he is miles behind modern 3-star heroes. For comparison, newer 3-star heroes like

boast power levels well over 530 and attack stats pushing 550–600+. Vlad is trying to fight in a weight class that he no longer belongs to; his defensive stats are passable for a classic 3-star, but his offensive presence is virtually non-existent by today's standards.
is a fascinatingly unique but practically flawed skill.
It deals fixed damage over time (DoT). At base, it's 65 damage over 5 turns. That is
. Even in 3-star tournaments, this is negligible. If you manage to fire him again on the same target, it refreshes and stacks up to 295 total damage (59 per turn).
This is his main gimmick. Stealing 61% of healing applied to the target is a cool mechanic to counter healers.

The setup required to make this work is immense. You need to hit a specific target, hope they are about to be healed, and hope that 61% of that healing (plus the tickle of DoT) actually matters. In a meta where battles are decided by massive alpha strikes or powerful buffs, waiting 5 turns for mediocre damage is a losing strategy.
Very Fast (6.5 tiles). This is his one saving grace, allowing him to charge in 2 matches (6 tiles) with a small amount of mana support (e.g., family bonus or emblems/troops).
A modern sniper hits for 300-400% upfront damage instantly. Vlad needs multiple casts and 5 turns to achieve what others do in one click.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
bonus provides nice perks like mana generation delays for enemies, but it requires multiple unique family members. While useful, it doesn't salvage Vlad's individual performance.

This is actually a very strong passive. A 65% chance to steal the newest dispellable buff from an enemy is excellent utility, especially at Very Fast speed. If no buffs are present, he deals 150% damage to all enemies (which, given his low attack stat, is still not a lot, but better than nothing). This passive is arguably better than his active skill.
Heals for a percentage of normal damage dealt. Since his attack stat is rock bottom, this healing is minimal and doesn't significantly boost his survivability.
When Vlad was first released, he was considered "niche" at best. Players experimented with him to counter heavy healing teams in 3-star arenas, but he never truly found a home in the meta.
He has been completely left behind. Modern 3-stars have passives, stats, and skills that do more in one turn than Vlad does in five.
He is currently one of the weakest event heroes in the game. His damage is too low to be a threat, and his utility is too situational.
Note: At the time of this review, Vlad does not have a costume.
If you insist on using Vlad (perhaps for a specific challenge or personal affection), you need to keep him alive long enough to cast multiple times.
Maximizes his survivability, allowing him to potentially stack his DoT and steal more buffs via his passive.
His attack stat—and thus his passive's "damage all" component and tile damage—remains pitiful.
. He is a support/disruptor, not a damage dealer. His DoT is fixed damage, so Attack emblems do not increase his special skill damage (only his tile damage and conditional passive damage).
Can charge quickly and potentially disrupt early game buffs.
The 65% chance to steal a buff is legitimate utility.
Can annoy teams relying heavily on a single healer (if aimed correctly).
His stats are ancient and crumble against modern heroes.
13 damage per turn is effectively nothing.
Requires multiple casts on the same target to reach decent damage numbers.
If the enemy doesn't heal, half his skill does nothing.
His Very Fast speed is wasted here, but he can spam his skill. However, better options usually exist.
If he steals a buff, he gains value. His passive shines here.
He looks nice next to other Halloween heroes.

You probably already ate him. If you have him in your roster, he is a relic. Do not Limit Break him. Do not give him Alpha Aethers. He is a candidate for the Soul Exchange if they ever accept 3-star heroes (they don't, so just feed him).
The community has never been kind to Vlad, and time has not healed those wounds.
"I usually keep at least one of each hero I get. Vlad is an exception... He is just terrible."
"Vlad is a disappointing rare hero. Unlike most 3* heroes... he doesn’t deserve the food and mats."
"Missing on Effect 1 is 'This effect can’t be dispelled.'
Sarcasm regarding how weak the effect is

One user tried to defend him: "Vlad managed to survive Li’s hit... and he stole Sabina’s healing long enough for me to kill Sabina." But they admitted, "only reason Vlad was even in that attack team, was that I had nobody better."

Vlad is a vampire who forgot to sharpen his fangs. While his concept of a very fast anti-healer is interesting, the numbers just aren't there. He does too little damage, too slowly, with stats that leave him incredibly fragile. Unless he receives a massive overhaul or a costume that rewrites his entire existence, he remains one of the weakest heroes in the game.
Don't let him suck the resources out of your roster.
A deep dive into Vlad, the 3-Star Dark hero from the Return to Morlovia event. We analyze his stats, 'Dawn of Sorrow' special skill, and current viability in the Empires & Puzzles meta.
